tool.name: Flatten collection
tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Flattens a nested collection into a simple list.
=========== Description ===========
This tool takes a nested collection — such as a list of lists (``list:list``), a list of dataset pairs (``list:paired``), or even deeper structures — and collapses it into a single flat ``list``. Every dataset in the hierarchy becomes a top-level element in the output.
Because the hierarchy is removed, the element identifiers from each nesting level are joined together to form new unique names. By default the separator is an underscore (``_``), but you can choose a colon (``:``) or dash (``-``) instead.
======== Examples ========
**Flatten a list of pairs**
A ``list:paired`` collection with two samples::
list:paired: "Sequencing reads"
sample1/ forward → reads_R1.fastq reverse → reads_R2.fastq sample2/ forward → reads_R1.fastq reverse → reads_R2.fastq
After flattening with separator ``_``::
list: "Sequencing reads (flattened)"
sample1_forward → reads_R1.fastq sample1_reverse → reads_R2.fastq sample2_forward → reads_R1.fastq sample2_reverse → reads_R2.fastq
-------
**Flatten a list of lists**
A ``list:list`` collection::
list:list: "Batches"
batch1/ Dataset 1 Dataset 2 batch2/ Dataset 3
After flattening::
list: "Batches (flattened)"
batch1_Dataset 1 batch1_Dataset 2 batch2_Dataset 3
-------
**Choosing a different separator**
With separator set to ``-`` instead of ``_``, the same example would produce ``batch1-Dataset 1``, ``batch1-Dataset 2``, etc.
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/flatten.svg :alt: Flatten a nested collection into a simple list :width: 500
-------
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
